violations.text: Raw Beef, Veal, Hamburgers, and Shrimp were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination/cross-contact in the prep unit and walk-in cooler. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross-contamination/ cross-contact is prevented. Raw animal food items were moved for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Chemicals were stored on the shelf above banquet equipment in the basement prep area. Proper storage was discussed at the time of inspection. Chemicals began being moved at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

An insect control light was observed stored on the wall above the basement prep area prep table. Insect control devices shall be installed so that the devices are not located over a FOOD preparation area, and so that dead insects and insect fragments are prevented from being impelled onto or falling on exposed FOOD, clean EQUIPMENT, UTENSILS, LINENS, and unwrapped SINGLE-SERVICE and SINGLE-USE ARTICLES. Prep area table was moved away from insect control light at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

At the time of inspection, canned goods and a box of sugar packets in the basement area were observed to be adulterated by rodent droppings. Canned goods and sugar packets were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection (See Disposal list). New Violation. Correct By: 03/01/2024

Food contact surfaces of the deli slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. (Slicer was properly cleaned at the time of inspection.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/01/2024

At the time of inspection, pasta was observed to be cooling in the prep unit in a tall covered container. Items prepared/cooked on site and set for cooling are to be set in containers no greater than four inches deep and arranged in the EQUIPMENT to provide maximum heat transfer through the container walls and loosely covered, or uncovered if protected from overhead contamination. Pasta was spread out in a shallow pan for more rapid cooling at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/01/2024
